{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-50157", "cvss": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-200", "name": "Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-598", "name": "Use of GET Request Method With Sensitive Query Strings"}], "description": "### Description
Applications built with the Auth0 Symphony SDK, using the Authorizer security authenticator to protect HTTP routes may accept OAuth 2.0 bearer access tokens provided through a URL query parameter, in addition to the standard Authorization header, which may increase the risk of access token exposure and replay against protected API endpoints.

### Resolution
Upgrade auth0/symfony to version 5.9.0 or greater.

### Acknowledgement

title: Auth0 Symfony SDK Accepted Bearer Tokens via URL Query Parameter
